_doc: Declarative field schema for AGENT (contract) forging. A contract creates the agent — its identity and DEFAULT parameters — and nothing else: goals/titles/acceptance live on missions (mission_fields.json), not here. forge.py loads this; both the terminal CLI and the chat wizard iterate it. Field order IS the elicitation order. `parse` names a FieldType strategy resolved via forge._FIELD_TYPES (str | csv | toolkit | importance | expiry). essential: asked in the simpler terminal forge; non-essential fields are asked only in the full flow and otherwise take their default. ask=false sets a constant without prompting.
header: ═ Forge an agent (contract) ═  ·  identity + defaults; a mission gives it a goal later
safeword_prompt: Agent is coherent. Sign with a SAFEWORD to seal it (blank to cancel)

intent._doc: Deterministic detection of 'the operator wants to forge an agent/contract'. A message triggers the wizard when it names a contract/agent AND a create-verb, but NOT a VM noun (so 'a vm to test smart contracts' is left to the AI). Data-driven so the trigger words live here.

wizard._doc: Behaviour knobs for the chat forge wizard (forge_chat.py). The password needs_input `type` stays 'password' in code (a wire-protocol constant the client keys on to mask input); only the prompt text is config here.

fields.prompt: Red lines — tools to forbid (comma-separated, blank for none)
Default reward — how much does a typical mission matter? [routine|important|critical]
Expiry — when does this agent expire? (YYYY-MM-DD, a duration like 30d, or blank for never)
